headline: Hungary moves from published 28.1 (May 3 prior cycle 29.7) to 37.5 — material positive trajectory delta of +9.4 against canonical published score on day 1 of the Magyar government. Hold-release event (sworn in 3pm May 9) provides first enacted-executive evidence: TV2 news director dismissed (concrete media-freedom action), EU flag flown at Parliament (enacted symbolic re-alignment), Sulyok dismissal demanded by May 31 (signaled accountability), €17B EU funds named priority with EU Commission cooperation confirmed (enacted external relationship), 'system change / service not rule' inaugural framing (INT signal). Developing band SUSTAINED (37.5 still below the 40.0 Functional floor by 2.5 points). NO band crossing this cycle. Per the prompt's caution: day-1 statements + minimal enacted change does not justify a full Functional placement; INT/ACC/EQU receive partial credit only because at least one enacted action (TV2 dismissal) crossed the signaled-to-enacted threshold per dimension. Sulyok still in office at scan close; statute repeals not yet introduced; 27 EU rule-of-law super-milestones still pending August 31. Medium confidence per the prompt's day-1 caution. Next material event: 30-day post-formation cycle (~June 9).
hold_release_event: Péter Magyar sworn in as PM at 3pm 2026-05-09; ends Orbán-Fidesz 16-year rule
key_evidence: May 9 2026 inauguration (in-window): Péter Magyar sworn in as PM at 3pm; inaugural address declared 'system change, not just government change' and framed office as 'service, not rule.'
May 9 2026 (in-window): TV2 news director dismissed; additional pro-Orbán presenters removed — first concrete enacted media-freedom action on day 1.
May 9 2026 (in-window): EU flag flown at Parliament — symbolic but enacted re-alignment posture; first executive act of the new government's external orientation.
May 9 2026 (in-window): Sulyok presidential dismissal demanded by May 31 — accountability signaled with deadline but not yet enacted.
May 9 2026 (in-window): €17B frozen EU funds named explicit top priority; EU Commission confirmed cooperative framework for fund release.
April 19 2026 (pre-window structural context): Magyar-EU rule-of-law talks produced 27 super-milestones with August 31 2026 deadline.
April 14 2026 (pre-window structural context): HRW issued 'Hungary New Government Needs to Restore Rule of Law' — provides rights-org baseline.
Two-thirds Tisza supermajority (141/199 seats) enables Fundamental Law amendments — institutional capacity for structural reform confirmed.

evidence.source: Al Jazeera — Peter Magyar sworn in as Hungary's PM
evidence.finding: Magyar sworn in 3pm May 9; inaugural address with 'system change' and 'service not rule' framing; Sulyok May 31 deadline; €17B frozen funds priority; EU flag flown.
Day-1 executive transitions documented; TV2 news director dismissal; pro-Orbán presenters removed.
Tisza supermajority capacity for Fundamental Law amendments; cabinet decentralization (16 ministries).

evidence.source: Euronews — EU and Magyar agree to work together for release of EU cash
evidence.finding: Pre-inauguration April EU meetings established 27 rule-of-law super-milestones with August 31 deadline; EU framework cooperative.

evidence.source: HRW — Hungary new government needs to restore rule of law
evidence.finding: Rights-org baseline for measuring post-transition rule-of-law restoration progress; structural reform agenda mapped.

evidence.source: Balkan Insight — Tisza victory and media freedom
evidence.finding: 80% of Hungarian media under Orbán-era control; structural media-freedom reform opportunity identified; TV2 dismissal supports operational follow-through.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 2.5 unchanged. No day-1 suffering-detection institutional changes; ministerial reorganization is structural but not yet operational on detection pathways. Anchor 2-3 sustained.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 2.5 (+0.5 from published 2.0; +0.5 from May 3 prior). E1 affective resonance: inaugural framing 'service not rule' is a public empathic-regard commitment; E4 validation: public-participation design at Kossuth Square. Moves toward anchor 3 but not yet operationally tested. Conservative +0.5 reflects signal-only credit for E1/E4; E2/E3/E5 unchanged.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 2.5 unchanged. AC1 responsiveness: no operational response-time changes day 1; AC4 resource mobilization: €17B EU funds priority is signal not yet enacted. Anchor 2-3 sustained.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 2.0 (+0.5 from published 1.5). EQ1 universality: TV2 news director dismissal indicates day-1 willingness to act on media-pluralism (a structural equity precondition); EQ3 bias awareness: pro-Orbán presenter removals are corrective action. But anti-LGBTQ statute remains in force; Lex-CEU and other minority-rights regressions not yet repealed; Lhotshampa-equivalent minority statutes unchanged. Modest +0.5 reflects single enacted action on media-pluralism while statute repeals remain pending.
scoring_rationale.BND: 3.0 (+0.5 from published 2.5; +0.3 from May 3 prior 2.7). B3 scope clarity: EU flag flown at Parliament + EU Commission cooperative framework confirmed = enacted re-alignment posture on day 1 (not merely signaled). Rule-of-law restoration scope publicly committed with milestones-deadline structure. Moves cleanly to anchor 3. B1 (sustainability) and B4 (refusal ethics) unchanged.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 2.5 (+0.5 from published 2.0). AB1 harm acknowledgment: inaugural rejection of prior administration's posture and Sulyok dismissal demand acknowledge prior regime accountability needs; AB2 correction willingness: TV2 director dismissal is day-1 enacted correction. AB3 transparency: HRW April 14 baseline document not yet acted upon. Conservative +0.5 — Sulyok still in office; statute repeals not yet introduced.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 3.0 (+0.5 from published 2.5; +0.2 from May 3 prior 2.8). S2 long-term impact: 27 EU rule-of-law super-milestones with August 31 deadline = published 4-month structural-change roadmap; S4 structural critique: explicit framing of Orbán-era as systemic problem requiring 'system change'; 16-ministry decentralization is operational structural-reform on day 1. Moves cleanly to anchor 3.
scoring_rationale.INT: 2.0 (+0.5 from published 1.5). I1 consistency under pressure: TV2 director dismissal is day-1 costly action against entrenched media-control architecture (will provoke Fidesz-aligned media backlash); I2 non-performance: public-participation inaugural design indicates non-reputational orientation; I5 resilience-of-care: leadership transition completed without observable degradation in window. Conservative +0.5 — values-action gap not yet tested under sustained pressure; INT remains the most pending dimension.

math_check.validator_check: Material delta (+9.4 vs published; >5pt materiality threshold). Sub-threshold from band crossing (37.5 is 2.5 points below Functional floor at 40.0). Developing band sustained. Not a boundary case (>1.0 from band boundary). Math reconstructs cleanly to 37.5.

band_crossing_outcome: NO. Composite 37.5 remains in Developing band (21-40). 2.5 points below Functional floor (40.0). Per prompt's caution: day-1 statements + minimal enacted change does not justify Functional placement; INT/ACC/EQU partial credit only where one or more enacted action crossed the signaled-to-enacted threshold per dimension.
boundary_flag: NOT a boundary case. 2.5 points below the 40.0 Functional floor; outside the 1.0pt boundary-flag window.
first_post_inauguration_judgment: Methodology v1.2 conservative-scoring rule applied. Hold-release event triggered re-assessment. Day-1 enacted actions (TV2 dismissal, EU flag, cabinet decentralization operational) justified partial dimensional movement on 5 of 8 dimensions (EMP, EQU, BND, ACC, SYS, INT). Three dimensions (AWR, ACT) held — no detection-infrastructure or operational-response changes on day 1. The +9.4 composite delta is the largest single-cycle positive movement in countries this rotation but it does NOT cross the band boundary. The conservative call: enacted day-1 actions justify partial credit for trajectory-confirming dimensions but full Functional placement requires sustained operational reform across June-August milestones.
negativeSignals: Sulyok still in office at scan close (dismissal demanded but not enacted); anti-LGBTQ statute remains in force; 27 EU super-milestones still pending August 31 deadline; 80% of media remains under Orbán-era ownership architecture despite TV2 director dismissal; new government untested under sustained political/financial/external pressure; Fidesz-aligned regional media and Fundamental Law parts not yet amended.
positiveSignals: Constitutional transfer of executive power completed without incident; TV2 news director dismissal = first concrete media-freedom enacted action; EU flag flown at Parliament; €17B frozen EU funds named top priority; EU Commission cooperative framework confirmed; cabinet decentralized to 16 ministries; two-thirds supermajority enables Fundamental Law amendments; HRW + EU + civil-society baseline measurement structures aligned with new government's stated agenda; public-participation inaugural design.
sector_context: Countries — Governance Transition. Hungary is the highest-confidence positive band-change candidate in countries index this rotation but the +9.4 cycle delta falls 2.5 short of the 40.0 Functional band boundary. Substantively analogous to a major democratic-restoration cycle similar to Poland's 2023-2024 Tusk transition trajectory at equivalent post-inauguration stages. The cycle confirms that day-1 enacted action can produce material movement but full band placement requires sustained operational reform.
open_actions_resolved: Hold released; first post-inauguration assessment completed
Day-1 enacted actions credited under EMP, EQU, BND, ACC, SYS, INT
AWR and ACT held pending operational evidence
Material upgrade proposal generated (+9.4 vs published)
open_actions_carried: Watch May 31 deadline: Sulyok presidential dismissal (accountability enactment)
Watch June 1-15: first ministerial actions on anti-LGBTQ statute repeal (EQU)
Watch June: accountability/anti-corruption institutional process establishment (ACC)
Watch June-August: progress against 27 EU rule-of-law super-milestones (SYS)
Watch August 31 deadline: EU rule-of-law milestone deadline (SYS/BND/ACC consolidation)
Boundary watch: if June 9 cycle continues positive trajectory, Functional band crossing possible
